title: Andrographolide modulates HNF4α activity imparting on hepatic metabolism

ai_summary.one_sentence_summary: 本研究发现穿心莲内酯（AP）通过直接结合HNF4α的Arg235残基，破坏其与辅激活因子PGC1α的相互作用，从而抑制HNF4α转录活性，下调ApoB、MTP、PLA2G12B、Pepck和G6pc等靶基因表达，最终减少肝脏VLDL分泌和糖异生，并在高脂饮食小鼠模型中改善高脂血症和肝脂肪变性。
ai_summary.background: HNF4α是调控肝脏发育和代谢的关键核受体，直接调控VLDL分泌（靶基因ApoB、MTP、PLA2G12B）和糖异生（靶基因Pepck、G6pc）相关基因表达。穿心莲内酯（AP）是穿心莲的主要活性成分，具有抗炎、保肝作用，但其调节糖脂代谢的分子机制尚不明确。
ai_summary.objective: 假设AP通过调控HNF4α活性影响肝脏代谢，本研究旨在验证AP作为HNF4α部分拮抗剂的具体作用机制，并阐明其对VLDL分泌和糖异生的调控效应。
ai_summary.methods: Gal4-HNF4α-LBD/PPFR-Luc双荧光素酶报告基因系统、分子对接（Glide XP、PDB 4B7W）、定点突变（R235L）、免疫共沉淀、原代小鼠肝细胞和HepG2细胞培养、BODIPY染色和脂滴定量、细胞内/分泌型TG测定、C57BL/6小鼠短期AP灌胃（100 mg/kg，5天）和高脂饮食HFD模型（AP 100 mg/kg，6周）、VLDL分泌实验（Triton WR1339）、口服葡萄糖耐量试验、生化指标（TG、ALT、AST、ApoB、TNFα、IL6）检测、qRT-PCR和Western blotting。
ai_summary.main_results: AP剂量依赖性抑制HNF4α-LBD/PGC1α介导的转录活性（IC50~25 μM），但不抑制LXRα。R235突变消除AP的抑制效应。AP在HEK293T细胞中破坏HNF4α与PGC1α的相互作用。AP降低ApoB、MTP、PLA2G12B启动子活性及HepG2细胞中这些基因的mRNA表达。在原代肝细胞中，AP减少脂滴面积和细胞内TG，并抑制TG分泌。短期AP处理小鼠降低肝脏VLDL分泌速率。在HFD小鼠中，AP降低血清TG、ApoB、ALT、AST，减轻肝脏脂肪变性和炎症（TNFα、IL6、CCL2下调），并下调Pepck、G6pc表达，改善糖耐量。
ai_summary.innovation: 首次揭示AP作为HNF4α直接拮抗剂的分子机制：通过结合HNF4α-LBD的Arg235残基阻断与PGC1α相互作用，从而抑制下游VLDL分泌和糖异生通路，为AP的降血脂和降糖活性提供新的机制解释。
ai_summary.limitations: 本研究主要基于小鼠模型和细胞实验，缺乏人体临床试验；未系统评估AP对除HNF4α外其他核受体的特异性；长期应用AP对肝脏代谢的全面影响（包括可能的不良作用）未深入探讨；AP的体内药代动力学特性及最佳给药方案未明确。
ai_summary.research_gap: 尽管论文明确了AP通过HNF4α-PGC1α通路调节脂质和糖代谢，但AP在缺血性脑卒中（尤其是脑缺血后血脂异常和糖代谢紊乱）中的潜在治疗作用尚未被探索；HNF4α通路是否参与脑卒中后神经损伤和修复仍不清楚。
ai_summary.project_relevance: 本课题聚焦金毛狗脊治疗缺血性脑卒中，该中药可能通过类似穿心莲内酯的代谢调节和抗炎机制发挥保护作用。论文所用的方法（虚拟筛选、分子对接、突变验证、细胞模型、体内药效学）可作为金毛狗脊活性成分研究的参考框架；HNF4α及其下游靶点（ApoB、Pepck等）可能是金毛狗脊治疗脑卒中的潜在干预环节，需进一步验证。
ai_summary.introduction_material: 可引用论文中关于HNF4α在肝脏代谢中核心作用的描述（引言第一段）、AP的传统抗炎及保肝活性（引言第二段）、以及AP对糖脂代谢影响机制未知的研究背景（引言末段），以支撑金毛狗脊研究中对代谢-炎症交叉调控的探讨。
ai_summary.discussion_material: 讨论部分可引用AP作为HNF4α拮抗剂的结合位点突变证据、AP对VLDL分泌和糖异生的双重抑制作用、AP在HFD小鼠中抗炎和改善NAFLD的表型、以及AP不激活LXRα显示的选择性，作为金毛狗脊类似机制讨论的参照。
recommended_experiments: 网络药理学预测金毛狗脊活性成分与HNF4α通路的关联
分子对接和表面等离子共振验证成分与HNF4α-LBD的结合
基于H/R（缺氧/复氧）细胞模型的神经保护实验，检测HNF4α通路基因表达
在MCAO（大脑中动脉闭塞）小鼠模型中评估金毛狗脊对神经功能、血脂、糖代谢和HNF4α下游靶点的影响
用Western blot和RT-qPCR验证关键蛋白（HNF4α、ApoB、Pepck）和炎症因子（TNFα、IL6）的变化
ai_summary.confidence_notes: 本JSON严格基于提供的论文文本，所有科学陈述直接源自原文实验数据和结论。项目相关性部分为合理推断，因论文未涉及金毛狗脊，故推荐靶点和通路源于论文发现的HNF4α及其效应分子，实验建议结合脑卒中模型设计，但需注意金毛狗脊与穿心莲内酯在化学结构上不同，其直接作用于HNF4α的假设需进一步验证。

Andrographolide modulates HNF4α activity imparting on hepatic
metabolism
Minyi Zhanga,b, Meng Yang b, Na Wang c, Qingli Liu b, Binxu Wang b, Tongling Huang b, Yan Tong d,
Yanlin Ming d, Chi-Wai Wong e, Jinsong Liu c, Dongsheng Yao a,∗∗, Min Guan b,∗

b Institute of Biomedicine and Biotechnology, Shenzhen Institutes of Advanced Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Shenzhen, 518055, Guangdong, China
c Guangdong Provincial Key Laboratory of Biocomputing, Guangzhou Institutes of Biomedicine and Health, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Guangzhou, 510530, China

ARTICLE INFO
Keywords:
Andrographolide
Hepatocyte nuclear factor 4 alpha
Transcriptional regulation
Very-low-density lipoprotein secretion
Gluconeogenesis
ABSTRACT
Hepatic nuclear factor 4 alpha (HNF4 α) drives the expression of apolipoprotein B (ApoB), microsomal trigly-
ceride transfer protein (MTP) and phospholipase A2 G12B (PLA2G12B), governing hepatic very-low-density
lipoprotein (VLDL) production and secretion. Andrographolide (AP) is a major constituent isolated from
Andrographis paniculata . We found that AP can disrupt the interaction between HNF4 α and its coactivator
peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma coactivator 1-alpha (PGC1 α). Virtual docking and mutational
analysis indicated that arginine 235 of HNF4 α is essential for binding to AP. As a consequence of antagonizing
the activity of HNF4 α, AP suppresses the expression of ApoB, MTP and PLA2G12B and reduces the rate of
hepatic VLDL secretion in vivo . AP additionally reduced gluconeogenesis via down-regulating the expression of
HNF4α target genes phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase (Pepck) and glucose-6-phosphatase (G6pc).
Collectively, our results suggest that AP a ﬀects liver function via modulating the transcriptional activity of
HNF4α.
1. Introduction
Hepatic nuclear factor 4 alpha (HNF4 α) is a nuclear hormone re-
ceptor essential for liver development ( Hang et al., 2017; Lau et al.,
2018; Thakur et al., 2019 ). HNF4 α governs major hepatic functions
through directly binding to response elements located adjacent to its
target genes and guiding their expression ( Gonzalez, 2008; Moore et al.,
2016; Sheena et al., 2005 ). Both carbohydrate and lipid metabolisms
are subjected to ﬁne-tuning by HNF4 α (Galcheva et al., 2019; Hayhurst
et al., 2001; Yamagata, 2014). Speci ﬁcally, HNF4 α controls hepatic
very-low-density lipoprotein (VLDL) production and secretion through
target genes apolipoprotein B ( ApoB), microsomal triglyceride transfer
protein ( MTP) and phospholipase A2 G12B ( PLA2G12B)( Dai and
Hussain, 2012; Guan et al., 2011 ; Wang and Burke, 2008). Additionally,
HNF4α reﬁnes the rate of gluconeogenesis by dictating the expression
of phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase (Pepck) and glucose-6-
phosphatase (G6pc) ( Chavalit et al., 2013; Xu et al., 2007).
Known for its bitter taste, Andrographis paniculata is used tradi-
tionally as a medicinal herb in India to ﬁght infection and in China to
alleviate fever associated with infection ( Hossain et al., 2014 ; Hu et al.,
2017). Andrographolide (AP) is a major constituent isolated from An-
drographis paniculata with strong anti-microbial and anti-in ﬂammatory
activities ( Abu-Ghefreh et al., 2009 ; Arifullah et al., 2013 ). Recent
pharmacological studies in animal models suggest that AP can also
confer protective function on the liver upon dietary- or toxin-induced
stress ( Cabrera et al., 2017 ; Thakur et al., 2016). Of note, both carbo-
hydrate and lipid metabolisms are speci ﬁcally modulated by AP; how-
ever, the mechanistic basis behind these e ﬀects are still largely un-
known.
Hyperlipidemia
is a major contributing factor to cardiovascular
diseases ( Merkler and Reiner, 2007 ). Excess dietary fat consumption is
believed to be one of the factors behind hyperlipidemia. Alteration in

hepatic triglyceride (TG)-rich VLDL (VLDL-TG) packing and secretion
can also result in dyslipidemia and other metabolic-related diseases ( Di
Filippo et al., 2014). Speci ﬁcally, dysfunctional VLDL synthesis and
release may be a factor behind the development of non-alcoholic fatty
acid diseases (NAFLD) ( Fujita et al., 2009). Statins and ﬁbrates are
prescribed for controlling hepatic cholesterol synthesis and lipid oxi-
dation for hyperlipidemia management ( Pahan, 2006). Other classes of
drugs, including natural products such as AP, that can modulate hepatic
lipid homeostasis may be further developed for hyperlipidemia or
NAFLD ( Islam, 2017 ).
Since AP can modulate liver function and that HNF4 α is a key factor
governing the rates of hepatic VLDL secretion and gluconeogenesis, we
hypothesize that the metabolic altering activity of AP is in part due to
its ability to modulate HNF4 α activity conferring some bene ﬁcial ef-
fects. In this study, we demonstrated that AP acts as a partial HNF4 α
antagonist, down-regulating the expression of ApoB, MTP, PLA2G12B,
Pepck, and G6pc; thereby, suppressing VLDL secretion and gluconeo-
genesis.
2. Materials and methods
2.1. Cloning and plasmids
The human HNF4 α-LBD fragment and human LXR α-LBD fragment
were subcloned into pCMV-BD vector to generate Gal4-HNF4 α LBD and
Gal4-LXRα LBD fusion constructs. pFR-Luciferase reporter plasmid was
purchased from Stratagene (Agilent Technologies, 211344). Human
PGC1α and mouse HNF4 α were cloned into pcDNA4 vector as pre-
viously reported ( Guan et al., 2011 ; Wang et al., 2009). The luciferase
reporter plasmids containing the mouse ApoB promoter ( −1981 to
+72), MTP promoter (−1823 to +135), PLA2G12B promoter (−1215
to +111) and G6pc promoter ( −934 to +22) were generated by PCR
and cloned into the Kpn Ⅰ (upstream) and XhoⅠ (downstream) sites of
pGL3 vector. The mouse Pepck promoter ( −1123 to +521) was sub-
cloned into pGL3 vector using Mlu Ⅰ and Xho Ⅰ restriction sites. Full
length mouse HNF4 α was subcloned into pcDNA3.1-HA vector. The
HNF4α-LBD mutant construct was generated by PCR-based mutagen-
esis using the QuikChange Site-Directed Mutagenesis Kit (Stratagene,
200518).
Primers used to generate plasmid constructs are listed in
Table 1.
2.2. Protein-ligand docking analysis
The published crystal structure of HNF4 α (PDB: 4B7W) was used for
modelling of possible binding modes. All crystallographic water mole-
cules were deleted. The crystal structure was prepared for docking
using the protein preparation wizard in Maestro (Version 9.6,
Schrödinger), which assigns bond orders and adds hydrogens and
missing atoms. The active site was deﬁ ned with Receptor Grid
Generation model. Glide was used for the protein-ligand docking in XP
protocol. Multiple stereoisomers, ionization states, and 3D structures of
AP as input to the docking calculation were initially generated by
LigPrep using the OPLS_2005 force ﬁeld. Docked binding modes were
ranked using the Docking Score and manually inspected for the rea-
sonable interaction. Docking ﬁgure was generated using PyMOL.
2.3. Primary hepatocytes isolation and cell culture
Primary hepatocytes were isolated from 8-week-old C57BL/6 mice
by collagenase perfusion and percoll gradient puri ﬁcation (Meyer et al.,
2016). Brie ﬂy, liver was perfused with collagenase type I solution
(Sigma, V900892), followed by digestion and ﬁltering of the cell sus-
pension through a 70- μm mesh (BD Falcon, 352350). Hepatocytes were
further puriﬁ ed by Percoll-gradient centrifugation (1000× g for
10 min at 4 °C). Viability of cells (~80%) was analyzed by trypan blue
staining. The cells were seeded on collagen-coated plates and main-
tained in DMEM (Hyclone, SH30021.01) supplemented with 10% FBS
(Corning, 35076-CV) and 1% penicillin/streptomycin solution (P/S)
(Hyclone, SV30010). HEK293T and HepG2 cells were obtained from
Type Culture Collection of the Chinese Academy of Sciences. HEK293T
cells were maintained in DMEM (Hyclone, SH30243.01) supplemented
with 10% FBS and 1% P/S. HepG2 cells were cultured in DMEM (Hy-
clone, SH30021.01) supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% P/S. Cells
were incubated at 37 °C under 5% CO
2 atmosphere. HepG2 cells were
treated with indicated doses of BI6015 (APExBIO, 93987-29-2,

2.4. Transient transfection and luciferase assays
HEK293T cells were transfected by using lipofectamine 3000
(Invitrogen, L3000015) with promoter plasmids (25 ng) together with
HNF4α (25 ng) and PGC1α (10 ng) expression vector for 6 h. Wild type
Gal4-HNF4α LBD (5 ng), mutant R235L (5 ng) or Gal4-LXR α-LBD (5 ng)
fusion constructs were transfected into HEK293T cells together with
pFR-Luciferase reporter plasmid (10 ng) or PGC1 α (5 ng) expression
vector for 6 h pRL-TK vector was co-transfected to monitor the trans-
fection e ﬃciency. Cells maintained in medium with 10% charcoal
stripped FBS were treated with 10, 25, 50 μM AP or DMSO (Sigma-
Aldrich, D2650) for 24 h after transfection. TO901317 (Sigma-Aldrich,
T2320, purity≥ 98%) was used as an agonist of LXR α. Luciferase ac-
tivity was measured with a dual-luciferase reporter assay system
(Promega, E1960) a
